definition: USD/JPY carry trade positioning ($500B-$1T estimated) functions as a regime-change accelerator that converts gradual correlation shifts into abrupt flips through forced deleveraging from yen-funded positions hitting both equity and bond markets via margin calls. The July 2024 episode provides out-of-sample confirmation: USD/JPY fell from ~162 to ~142, VIX spiked to 38, and stock-bond correlation temporarily jumped to +0.5.
evidence: July 2024 episode: USD/JPY 162→142, VIX to 38, S&P -5-6%, correlation spike to +0.5
Carry positioning estimated at $500B-$1T (not directly observable)
July 2024 positive correlation lasted only days — amplifier vs. cause distinction
open_questions: What is actual carry trade sizing (not directly observable)?
Does BOJ communication under Ueda reduce discontinuity risk vs. Kuroda era?
Is this an amplifier of existing regime shifts or can it independently trigger one?
